QF.id: Evaluating Fund Performance and Fund Selection_46

question.text: Kumar's fund returned 12% while the peer group average was 10% and the benchmark returned 11%. How should his fund's performance be evaluated?

answer.text: Poor performance because 12% is low in absolute terms
Average performance as it only slightly beat the benchmark
Good performance as it outperformed both the benchmark and peer group average
Performance cannot be evaluated without knowing the risk-free rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Performance evaluation uses multiple frames — benchmark comparison (12% > 11% = outperformed) and peer group comparison (12% > 10% = above average). The fund excelled on both measures. **The Trap:** Candidates may focus on absolute returns alone or think only benchmark comparison matters, ignoring peer group performance. **Cross-Exam Context:** The textbook emphasizes evaluating fund performance relative to both benchmark and peer group, not just in isolation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Present fund performance in context — show benchmark comparison AND peer group ranking together. A fund beating both stands out as genuinely well-managed.
